transcript: Yeah! Chris, what’s up, man? This is Donnie Rollon. I met ya over at Yarmouth. Was fishin’ the one time and, ah, buddy, I think you may have, uh, taken some of my fish most recently when I was up ‘ere.
I don’t kno—I don’t kno—I don’t know what you’re talkin’ about. Whadda ya mean I “might’ve tooken some of your fish”?
I just wanna get ‘em back, is what I mean. You had ‘em and you put ‘em in your truck and you took off—
—Well, I think you got the wrong fuckin’ number!—
—No! No! No, bud! You got the wrong fuckin’ number! Don’t you start accusin’ me of fuckin’ sweet shit!
Hey, I was there too, guy! I saw what happened. Ya got me?
Who the fuck are you looking for?—
—Who are you looking for, to start with?
—for Chris! I met ya at Yarmouth fair ’n square! Guy!
I was fishin’ there all day!—
—I got off the ferry and I was fishin’ all day long. You come along and you take my freakin’ fish and you took off in your truck! What do you want from me? I just want ‘em back! [inhales]
I never took your fuckin’ fish!
Just give ‘em back! That’s all’s I gotta say!—
—Listen, I don’t know who the fuck you’re talkin’ about! Go fuck yourself!
Ah—want me to push you around? Whadda ya think of that? [exhales]—
—Sure! Sure! Go for it, man! ‘Cause I don’t got a clue who you are! I have no idea what you’re talking about!
You took my freakin’ fish, tough guy! [exhales] [sfx]—
—I took your fish? I took your fish? Justin, do you know some “Donnie” that I sold fish? Did I sell you fish?
Donnie Rollon; that’s my name. You took my frekin’ fish, guy! And I had eight, ten of ‘em sittin’ there. Now I got nothin’! You scoop ‘em up and you took off in your freakin’ truck!—

—I didn’t d—no! I did not! Somebody’s use—use—you know what? Somebody’s usin’ a false number aggravatin’ me. ‘Cause I’m gonna tell ya why; I didn’t—Justin, this fool’s tryin’ to say I pulled up to his truck—Yarmouth! Some “Donnie Roland”!—and took ten fish off his tailboard! Yo, I hate fish! You go fuck yourself, you piece of shit!
I’m gonna push you down—how’s that sound?—next time I see ya, tough stuff! [delay]
Hey, guy, I’m gonna throw ya out the window! How’s that sound to ya?
Well I hope you do! Honestly, ‘cause I—
—honestly don’t got a clue who you are. I’m out scollop draggin’ right now in the Bay of Fundy! Believe me, I ain’t been nowhere near your fucking truck!
I’m gonna drive your truck off a cliff and throw ya through the window!
You’re gonna s—you know what you can do—
—ah, Mr. Peacock? You can suck me the fuck off! Goodbye!
Hey, buddy, listen: ah, just a hundred dollars for the fish—you reimburse me—and we’ll call it good. This is Donnie, okay? Donnie Rollon, here. I can stop by and pick it up; that’s no problem!
You come right to my house and come get it, bud. I’ll be waitin’ for ya—
I just wanna work it out, alright?

Oh, you’re gonna work it out! You—come—come to my house. Come pick it up. House number 6939. Pull right out back of the household, bud. I got somethin’ for ya. I got your hundred dollars and a little surprise. Come on up! Just to be a nice fella! [inhales] And I’m gonna tell ya somethin’, Mister Peacock or Hellfrog; you keep fuckin’ sending prank fuckin’ phone calls to me and I promise you, if I ever find out, I’ll rip your fukcin’ throat out. So you come right up to my house, right now. Come on, Hellfrog or Peacock! Come on up! I’ll be waitin’!
[pitch-up] I’ll promise you a bandagin’. [pitch-down] I’ll bandage you from head to toe, ya feel me? [delay]—
—‘Cause I ain’t fiddlin’ around, guy! You mess with a man’s fish—
—“Fiddlin’ around”? Come on up! I like ta fiddle around with ya! Come on!
[pitch-up] Hey! [pitch-down] You fiddle with a man’s fish, you’re fiddlin’ with his livelihood!—
—“Fiddle with a man’s fish”! I never took your fish!—
—[pitch-up] Ya understand? Buddy, you’re fiddlin’ with my fish! I saw ya do it!
Ah, you know what, man? Suck my fuckin’ dick! Goodbye!
I’ll shove ya into the ground! I’m tired o’ messin’ with you.
Buddy, why don’t you just send me an online payment—a hundred dollars? And then we’ll call it good!—
—‘Cause I’m tired o’ messin’ around here!—
—shove them fish up your ass, eyeballs first?
I don’t have my frekin’ fish, tough guy! You took ‘em and split! I was there!
Yeah, so I—got ‘em—got ‘em—I got ‘em right here in the fridge!
Yeah! So you know—you’re damn right you know I do! That’s why I’m telling you, come on up and get ‘em and I’ll stuff ‘em up your ass! How would you like ‘em, tail-first or eyeballs?
You don’t know how to fish, is that it? You’re no damn good—
Oh, I don’t use a fishin’ rod, bud. I use a scollop drag.
I’m gonna use my bare hands.
I wish you would! I’d like for you to come up!

[dynamic pitch] Whadda ya think of that? And then I’m gonna take ya down! I’m gonna show ya how to fish! I’m gonna show ya how to fish, first-hand, from the master, ya underst—[delay]—‘cause I’m not kiddin’ around up [delay & pitch-up] Just send me an online payment! Quit jokin’ around!—
—Yeah, alright, okay! Yeah, gimme some numbers! Let me send it to ya. And that way you ain’t gotta keep callin’ my fucking number!
Hi, this is Trout Waters. How’s it goin’?
Who is this? I have no idea who you are.
We’re Trout Waters Fly & Tackle. Your buddy said you picked up someone else’s fish.
Oh, go fuck off! You know that? You go fuck off, drone. I’ll see you in your gunsight before you fucking know it, buddy!
[to LPC ] He just told me to “f off” ‘cause he didn’t know who this was.
